requiredFiles.reason: Update the shared response-details open/close state function so it synchronizes the existing details toggle button's aria-expanded value with the panel visibility for both toggle-open and close-button actions, without changing watch behavior.
Add an independently runnable unittest regression using tests.frontend_harness.run_dashboard to verify response-details visibility and aria-expanded state after opening and closing.

acceptanceCriteria: When response details are opened, the details panel is visible and the existing #detailsToggle button reports aria-expanded="true".
When response details are closed through #detailsClose, the details panel is hidden and #detailsToggle reports aria-expanded="false".
The details toggle continues to derive opening behavior from the panel's current hidden state.
Existing watch behavior is preserved: clicking #watchIncident changes its watching dataset value to "true" and sets #watchStatus to "Watching incident".
A frontend regression test is added under tests/generated/ and uses tests.frontend_harness.run_dashboard.
implementationChecklist: Modify setDetailsOpen(open) to set both #detailsPanel.hidden to !open and #detailsToggle aria-expanded to String(open).
Keep both the #detailsToggle and #detailsClose click handlers routed through setDetailsOpen so both interaction paths update accessibility state.
Do not alter the watch button listener or its dataset/status text behavior.

Add a unittest that separately verifies the opened state after ["#detailsToggle"] and the closed state after ["#detailsToggle", "#detailsClose"], asserting both detailsHidden and detailsExpanded harness fields.
Do not modify tests/baseline/ or tests/oracle/.
verificationChecklist: Harness execution with ["#detailsToggle"] returns detailsHidden as false and detailsExpanded as "true".
Harness execution with ["#detailsToggle", "#detailsClose"] returns detailsHidden as true and detailsExpanded as "false".
Existing baseline watch test still returns watching as "true" and watchStatus as "Watching incident" after a watch-button click.
The generated regression test runs through the existing Node-backed frontend harness without browser or network dependencies.
